notes: This manifest records safety boundaries for TUN helper work. It does not authorize local-machine network changes and does not select a real test host.

mutation_slices.notes: The developer workstation must not be used for TUN, route, DNS, firewall, proxy, VPN, or network-service mutation tests.

Temporary TUN device creation has approved-VM smoke coverage, including read-only preflight plus the gated CLI helper Phase A smoke with rollback journal, retained-journal recovery, and default-route/global-DNS unchanged checks, and still requires explicit per-run approval.

Documentation-prefix route mutation has approved-VM smoke coverage, including read-only preflight plus the gated CLI helper Phase A smoke with rollback journal, retained-journal recovery, and default-route/global-DNS unchanged checks, and must not be generalized to default-route or production-route mutation without a separate review.
Namespace-scoped DNS smoke coverage exists; global DNS policy remains blocked.
Global DNS mutation is blocked until policy, platform behavior, rollback, and leak tests are defined.
Service-manager lifecycle smoke has approved-VM coverage through transient systemd units for success and failure cleanup paths. It remains approved-host-only and does not install permanent units.

Leak/coexistence smoke has approved-VM coverage for namespace default/DNS routing to TUN, preserved control-plane routing, TCP echo coexistence, host listener/default-route/global-DNS baselines, rollback, and residue checks.
